data.content:
### Use inputs Context in Manually Triggered Workflow

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/contexts

Shows a workflow triggered by workflow_dispatch event that accepts user-provided inputs through the inputs context. Defines build_id and deploy_target as strings and perform_deploy as boolean, with conditional job execution based on the perform_deploy input value.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      build_id:
        required: true
        type: string
      deploy_target:
        required: true
        type: string
      perform_deploy:
        required: true
        type: boolean

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ inputs.perform_deploy }}
    steps:
      - name: Deploy build to target
        run: echo "Deploying build:${{ inputs.build_id }} to target:${{ inputs.deploy_target }}"
```

--------------------------------

### Define Inputs for Manual Workflow Dispatch

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/write-workflows/choose-when-workflows-run/trigger-a-workflow

Configure the `workflow_dispatch` event with typed input parameters including choice, boolean, string, and environment types. Inputs are passed to the workflow and accessible via the `inputs` context. Maximum 25 top-level properties and 65,535 character payload limit apply.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
          - info
          - warning
          - debug
      print_tags:
        description: 'True to print to STDOUT'
        required: true
        type: boolean
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: true
        type: string
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  print-tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ inputs.print_tags }}
    steps:
      - name: Print the input tag to STDOUT
        run: echo  The tags are ${{ inputs.tags }}
```

--------------------------------

### Define Workflow Inputs with workflow_dispatch

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/events-that-trigger-workflows

Configure custom input properties with types, default values, and required flags for the workflow_dispatch event. This example demonstrates defining logLevel (choice), tags (boolean), and environment (environment type) inputs that can be accessed via the inputs context during workflow execution.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
        - info
        - warning
        - debug
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: false
        type: boolean
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  log-the-inputs:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: |
          echo "Log level: $LEVEL"
          echo "Tags: $TAGS"
          echo "Environment: $ENVIRONMENT"
        env:
          LEVEL: ${{ inputs.logLevel }}
          TAGS: ${{ inputs.tags }}
          ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.environment }}
```

--------------------------------

### Define Manual Workflow Inputs with workflow_dispatch Event

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/writing-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Configure on.workflow_dispatch.inputs to accept manual parameters when triggering a workflow. Supports multiple input types including choice, boolean, string, and environment. Inputs are accessible via the inputs context and have a maximum payload of 65,535 characters with up to 25 top-level properties.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
          - info
          - warning
          - debug
      print_tags:
        description: 'True to print to STDOUT'
        required: true
        type: boolean
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: true
        type: string
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  print-tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ inputs.print_tags }}
    steps:
      - name: Print the input tag to STDOUT
        run: echo The tags are ${{ inputs.tags }}
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions Workflow Dispatch Inputs

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/writing-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Defines custom input parameters for workflows triggered manually using the `workflow_dispatch` event. These inputs can be accessed in the workflow via the `inputs` context.

```APIDOC
## CONFIGURATION on.workflow_dispatch.inputs

### Description
Configures input fields for a workflow that can be triggered manually via `workflow_dispatch`. These inputs are passed to the workflow and are available in the `inputs` context. The maximum number of top-level properties for `inputs` is 25, and the maximum payload is 65,535 characters.

### Method
Configuration

### Endpoint
on.workflow_dispatch.inputs

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **<input_id>** (object) - Required - A string identifier for the input.
  - **description** (string) - Required - A brief description of the input.
  - **required** (boolean) - Required - Specifies whether the input is mandatory.
  - **default** (string) - Optional - The default value for the input if not provided.
  - **type** (string) - Required - The type of input. Supported types include `string`, `boolean`, `choice`, `environment`.
  - **options** (array of strings) - Optional (for `choice` type) - A list of allowed string values if `type` is `choice`.

### Request Example
```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
          - info
          - warning
          - debug
      print_tags:
        description: 'True to print to STDOUT'
        required: true
        type: boolean
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: true
        type: string
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  print-tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ inputs.print_tags }}
    steps:
      - name: Print the input tag to STDOUT
        run: echo  The tags are ${{ inputs.tags }}
```

### Response
N/A
```

--------------------------------

### Inputs Context for Workflow Dispatch

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/accessing-contextual-information-about-workflow-runs

The inputs context contains input properties passed to an action, reusable workflow, or manually triggered workflow. Input names and types are defined in the workflow configuration and accessed during workflow execution.

```APIDOC
## Inputs Context

### Description
The `inputs` context contains input properties passed to an action, reusable workflow, or manually triggered workflow. For reusable workflows, inputs are defined in the `workflow_call` event configuration. For manually triggered workflows, inputs are defined in the `workflow_dispatch` event configuration.

### Context Properties
- **inputs** (object) - Required - Container for all input values passed to the workflow
- **inputs.<name>** (string | number | boolean | choice) - Required - Individual input value passed from an external workflow or workflow_dispatch event

### Availability
- Available in reusable workflows called via `workflow_call`
- Available in workflows triggered by `workflow_dispatch` event
- Accessible from any job or step in a workflow

### Usage Pattern
Access input values using: `${{ inputs.<name> }}`

### Property Types
Each input value can be one of the following types:
- **string** - Text input value
- **number** - Numeric input value
- **boolean** - True/False input value
- **choice** - Selection from predefined options

### Configuration Example
Inputs are defined in the workflow file under `workflow_dispatch` or `workflow_call` event configuration and are passed from external workflows or manual triggers.
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions: Define Inputs for Manually Triggered Workflow Dispatch

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/writing-workflows/choosing-when-your-workflow-runs/triggering-a-workflow

This GitHub Actions workflow demonstrates how to define various input types (choice, boolean, string, environment) for a `workflow_dispatch` event, enabling users to provide parameters when manually triggering the workflow. It includes a job that consumes one of these inputs, `inputs.tags`, and prints its value, illustrating how to access user-provided data within the workflow. Note that inputs are available in both `inputs` and `github.event.inputs` contexts.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
          - info
          - warning
          - debug
      print_tags:
        description: 'True to print to STDOUT'
        required: true
        type: boolean
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: true
        type: string
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  print-tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ inputs.print_tags }} 
    steps:
      - name: Print the input tag to STDOUT
        run: echo  The tags are ${{ inputs.tags }} 

```

--------------------------------

### Define Workflow Inputs for Manual Trigger (GitHub Actions YAML)

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/events-that-trigger-workflows

This snippet demonstrates how to define custom inputs such as 'logLevel', 'tags', and 'environment' for a GitHub Actions workflow that is manually triggered via `workflow_dispatch`. It specifies input properties like description, required status, default values, and data types (e.g., 'choice', 'boolean', 'environment'), then accesses these inputs within a job using the `inputs` context.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
        - info
        - warning
        - debug
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: false
        type: boolean
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  log-the-inputs:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: |
          echo "Log level: $LEVEL"
          echo "Tags: $TAGS"
          echo "Environment: $ENVIRONMENT"
        env:
          LEVEL: ${{ inputs.logLevel }}
          TAGS: ${{ inputs.tags }}
          ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.environment }}

```

--------------------------------

### Configuring `workflow_dispatch` with Inputs

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/events-that-trigger-workflows

This section details how to define custom inputs for workflows that are manually triggered via `workflow_dispatch`, allowing users to provide runtime parameters.

```APIDOC
## YAML Configuration for `workflow_dispatch`

### Description
Configures a GitHub Actions workflow to be manually triggered (`workflow_dispatch`) and defines custom inputs that can be provided at runtime.

### Method
YAML Configuration

### Endpoint
GitHub Actions Event Listener

### Parameters
#### Request Body (Workflow Configuration)
- **on.workflow_dispatch.inputs.logLevel** (choice) - Required - 'Log level'. Available options: 'info', 'warning', 'debug'. Default: 'warning'.
- **on.workflow_dispatch.inputs.tags** (boolean) - Optional - 'Test scenario tags'.
- **on.workflow_dispatch.inputs.environment** (environment) - Required - 'Environment to run tests against'.

### Request Example
```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
        - info
        - warning
        - debug
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: false
        type: boolean
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  log-the-inputs:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: |
          echo "Log level: $LEVEL"
          echo "Tags: $TAGS"
          echo "Environment: $ENVIRONMENT"
        env:
          LEVEL: ${{ inputs.logLevel }}
          TAGS: ${{ inputs.tags }}
          ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.environment }}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (Workflow Triggered)
- Workflow is dispatched and executes based on the provided inputs.

#### Response Example
```text
Workflow run initiated successfully.
```
```

--------------------------------

### Use inputs Context in Reusable Workflow

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/contexts

Demonstrates a reusable workflow that defines and accesses inputs through the inputs context. The workflow accepts build_id (number), deploy_target (string), and perform_deploy (boolean) inputs, then uses them in a conditional job and echo command to deploy a build to a target system.

```yaml
name: Reusable deploy workflow
on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      build_id:
        required: true
        type: number
      deploy_target:
        required: true
        type: string
      perform_deploy:
        required: true
        type: boolean

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ inputs.perform_deploy }}
    steps:
      - name: Deploy build to target
        run: echo "Deploying build:${{ inputs.build_id }} to target:${{ inputs.deploy_target }}"
```

--------------------------------

### Workflow Dispatch Input Properties

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/workflow-syntax

This section describes the properties available for configuring individual inputs within a GitHub Actions `workflow_dispatch` event. It covers how to specify if an input is required and its expected data type.

```APIDOC
## `on.workflow_dispatch.inputs.<input_id>` Configuration

### Description
This documentation describes the available properties for defining custom inputs in a GitHub Actions `workflow_dispatch` event. These properties allow you to control the behavior and validation of inputs provided when manually triggering a workflow.

### Resource Path
`on.workflow_dispatch.inputs.<input_id>`

### Properties
#### `required`
- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Description**: A boolean specifying whether the input must be supplied when triggering the workflow.
- **Required**: Optional

#### `type`
- **Type**: `string`
- **Description**: The data type of the input.
- **Required**: Optional
- **Allowed Values**: `boolean`, `choice`, `number`, `environment`, `string`

### Example Configuration (YAML)
```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      my-string:
        description: 'Reviewer to assign'
        required: true
        type: string
      my-number:
        description: 'Number of something'
        required: false
        type: number
      my-boolean:
        description: 'Enable feature'
        required: true
        type: boolean
```
```

--------------------------------

### Configure workflow run name with expressions in GitHub Actions

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/automating-your-workflow-with-github-actions/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Sets a dynamic run name for workflow executions using GitHub expressions and context variables. The run-name can include the deployment target input and the actor who triggered the workflow. Supports github and inputs contexts.

```yaml
run-name: Deploy to ${{ inputs.deploy_target }} by @${{ github.actor }}
```

--------------------------------

### on.workflow_dispatch Manual Trigger with Inputs

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Defines custom inputs for manually triggering a workflow via `workflow_dispatch` in the GitHub UI, allowing users to provide custom values when running the workflow.

```APIDOC
## CONFIGURATION on.workflow_dispatch.inputs

### Description
This configuration allows you to define custom input parameters for workflows triggered manually using `workflow_dispatch`. These inputs appear as form fields in the GitHub UI and can be used to customize workflow behavior.

### Method
Configuration

### Endpoint
on.workflow_dispatch.inputs

### Parameters
#### Request Body (YAML Structure)
- **<input_id>** (object) - Required - A unique string identifier for the input parameter.
  - **description** (string) - Required - A brief description of the input parameter shown to the user.
  - **required** (boolean) - Required - Specifies whether the input must be supplied by the user.
  - **default** (string) - Optional - The default value for the input if none is provided by the user.
  - **type** (string) - Required - The data type of the input. Supported types include `string`, `boolean`, `choice`, `environment`.
  - **options** (array of strings) - Optional - For `choice` type, a list of allowed string values that the user can select from.

### Request Example
```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
          - info
          - warning
          - debug
      print_tags:
        description: 'True to print to STDOUT'
        required: true
        type: boolean
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: true
        type: string
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  print-tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ inputs.print_tags }}
    steps:
      - name: Print the input tag to STDOUT
        run: echo  The tags are ${{ inputs.tags }}
```

### Notes
The triggered workflow receives inputs in the `inputs` context. The maximum number of top-level properties for `inputs` is 25, and the maximum payload for `inputs` is 65,535 characters. This trigger only receives events when the workflow file is on the default branch.
```

--------------------------------

### Define Reusable Workflow Inputs with Type and Default Values

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/automating-your-workflow-with-github-actions/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Shows how to use on.workflow_call.inputs to define typed input parameters for a reusable workflow. Each input requires a type parameter (boolean, number, or string) and can have optional description, default value, and required flag. Inputs are accessed within the workflow using the inputs context.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      username:
        description: 'A username passed from the caller workflow'
        default: 'john-doe'
        required: false
        type: string

jobs:
  print-username:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Print the input name to STDOUT
        run: echo The username is ${{ inputs.username }}
```

--------------------------------

### Run Workflow with Inputs Using GitHub CLI

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/events-that-trigger-workflows

Pass input values to a workflow_dispatch triggered workflow using the GitHub CLI gh command. Inputs are provided using -f flags with key=value pairs for logLevel, tags, and environment parameters.

```bash
gh workflow run run-tests.yml -f logLevel=warning -f tags=false -f environment=staging
```

--------------------------------

### Pass Inputs to Reusable Workflows in GitHub Actions

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/automating-your-workflow-with-github-actions/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Provide input values to called workflows using the `with` keyword. Inputs must match input specifications defined in the called workflow and are accessible via the `inputs` context rather than as environment variables.

```yaml
jobs:
  call-workflow:
    uses: octo-org/example-repo/.github/workflows/called-workflow.yml@main
    with:
      username: mona
```

--------------------------------

### Triggering `workflow_dispatch` via GitHub CLI

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/events-that-trigger-workflows

Demonstrates how to manually trigger a `workflow_dispatch` enabled workflow using the GitHub CLI, passing required and optional inputs as command-line arguments.

```APIDOC
## CLI Command `gh workflow run`

### Description
Triggers a workflow that has `workflow_dispatch` configured, providing inputs directly through the GitHub CLI.

### Method
CLI Command

### Endpoint
GitHub CLI

### Parameters
#### Command-line Arguments
- **run-tests.yml** (string) - Required - The name of the workflow file to run.
- **-f logLevel** (string) - Required - Sets the 'logLevel' input for the workflow.
- **-f tags** (boolean) - Optional - Sets the 'tags' input for the workflow.
- **-f environment** (string) - Required - Sets the 'environment' input for the workflow.

### Request Example
```bash
gh workflow run run-tests.yml -f logLevel=warning -f tags=false -f environment=staging
```

### Response
#### Success Response (CLI Output)
- Confirmation that the workflow run has been initiated.

#### Response Example
```text
✓ Workflow run started
To see the run: https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/actions/runs/1234567890
```
```

--------------------------------

### POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/workflows/{workflow_file_name}/dispatches

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/managing-workflow-runs/manually-running-a-workflow

Triggers a workflow configured with the `workflow_dispatch` event. This allows you to manually run a workflow in a repository, providing specific inputs and selecting the branch.

```APIDOC
## POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/workflows/{workflow_file_name}/dispatches

### Description
This endpoint allows you to trigger a workflow run manually. The workflow must be configured to use the `workflow_dispatch` event. You can specify the branch to run on and provide custom inputs to the workflow.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/workflows/{workflow_file_name}/dispatches`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **owner** (string) - Required - The account owner of the repository.
- **repo** (string) - Required - The name of the repository.
- **workflow_file_name** (string) - Required - The name of the workflow file. For example, `blank.yml`.

#### Request Body
- **ref** (string) - Required - The git reference (e.g., a branch name like `main` or `feature-branch`, or a tag name) for the workflow run.
- **inputs** (object) - Optional - Key-value pairs of inputs to pass to the workflow. The workflow file must define these inputs. Up to 25 inputs are supported.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "ref": "main",
  "inputs": {
    "name": "Mona Lisa",
    "greeting": "Hello world",
    "environment": "production"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (204 No Content)
This endpoint typically returns a `204 No Content` status upon successful dispatch, indicating that the request was processed and the workflow run has been queued or started.

#### Response Example
```
(No content)
```
```

--------------------------------

### Workflow Dispatch Input - Required Property

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/automating-your-workflow-with-github-actions/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Configure whether a workflow dispatch input is required. This boolean property controls if the input must be supplied when manually triggering the workflow.

```APIDOC
## Workflow Dispatch Input Required Property

### Description
Specifies whether a workflow dispatch input parameter must be supplied when manually triggering the workflow.

### Property
`on.workflow_dispatch.inputs.<input_id>.required`

### Type
Boolean

### Values
- `true` - The input must be supplied when triggering the workflow
- `false` - The input is optional when triggering the workflow (default)

### Usage Example
```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      environment:
        description: 'Deployment environment'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - production
          - staging
```

### Notes
- When set to `true`, the workflow trigger UI will require the user to provide a value
- When set to `false`, the input is optional and users can leave it blank
```

--------------------------------

### Access Context Properties Using Index and Property Syntax

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/contexts-reference

Demonstrates two equivalent methods for accessing context information in GitHub Actions expressions. Index syntax uses bracket notation while property dereference uses dot notation. Property names must start with a letter or underscore and contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, or underscores for property dereference syntax.

```text
Index syntax: github['sha']
Property dereference syntax: github.sha
```

--------------------------------

### Workflow Dispatch Input Configuration

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Configure input parameters for manually triggered GitHub Actions workflows. Define whether inputs are required and specify their data types to ensure proper input validation and handling.

```APIDOC
## Workflow Dispatch Inputs Configuration

### Description
Define and configure input parameters for GitHub Actions workflows triggered manually via workflow_dispatch event.

### Configuration Parameters

#### on.workflow_dispatch.inputs.<input_id>.required
- **Type**: Boolean
- **Required**: No
- **Default**: false
- **Description**: A boolean specifying whether the input must be supplied when manually triggering the workflow. When set to true, the workflow cannot be dispatched without providing a value for this input.

#### on.workflow_dispatch.inputs.<input_id>.type
- **Type**: String
- **Required**: No
- **Default**: string
- **Description**: Specifies the data type of the input. This must be one of the following values: `boolean`, `choice`, `number`, `environment`, or `string`.
- **Valid Values**:
  - `boolean` - A true/false value
  - `choice` - A selection from predefined options
  - `number` - A numeric value
  - `environment` - An environment name
  - `string` - A text string value

### Configuration Example
```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      environment:
        type: environment
        required: true
        description: "Select deployment environment"
      debug_mode:
        type: boolean
        required: false
        description: "Enable debug logging"
      version:
        type: choice
        required: true
        options:
          - v1.0
          - v2.0
          - v3.0
      build_number:
        type: number
        required: false
        description: "Build number for tracking"
      custom_message:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Custom deployment message"
```

### Notes
- The `required` parameter ensures mandatory input validation at workflow dispatch time
- The `type` parameter enables proper UI rendering and input validation in the GitHub Actions interface
- When `required` is true, the workflow dispatch UI will prevent execution without providing the input value
```

--------------------------------

### jobs.<job_id>.steps[*].if

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Applies conditional logic to prevent a step from running unless a specified condition is met. Supports all workflow contexts and expressions with optional ${{ }} syntax.

```APIDOC
## jobs.<job_id>.steps[*].if

### Description
You can use the `if` conditional to prevent a step from running unless a condition is met. You can use any supported context and expression to create a conditional.

### Parameters
- **if** (string) - Required - Expression that evaluates to true or false to determine step execution

### Expression Syntax
- **Optional ${{ }} syntax** - GitHub Actions automatically evaluates the `if` conditional as an expression in most cases
- **Required ${{ }} syntax** - Must always use when:
  - The expression starts with `!` (since `!` is reserved in YAML)
  - Using special YAML characters that require escaping

### Escaping Rules
When the expression starts with `!`, you must use one of these formats:
- `${{ ! startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') }}`
- `if: '! startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')'`
- `if: "! startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')"`
- `if: (! startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/'))`

### Supported Contexts
See Contexts reference for complete list of available contexts in `if` conditionals.

### Example Configurations
```yaml
steps:
  - name: Deploy to production
    if: ${{ ! startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') }}
    run: npm run deploy

  - name: Publish release
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')
    run: npm run publish

  - name: Run on specific branch
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    run: echo "Running on main branch"
```

### Related Documentation
For more information, see [Evaluate expressions in workflows and actions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/expressions)
```

--------------------------------

### Workflow Run Name Context Configuration

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/contexts-reference

Defines the available contexts and functions for the run-name workflow key. The run-name context allows access to github, inputs, and vars contexts only.

```APIDOC
## Workflow Key: run-name

### Description
Configuration point for setting the workflow run name in GitHub Actions.

### Available Contexts
- **github** - GitHub context information
- **inputs** - Workflow inputs
- **vars** - Environment variables

### Available Special Functions
None

### Usage Example
```yaml
run-name: ${{ github.actor }} - ${{ inputs.deployment-env }}
```

### Restrictions
The `secrets` context is NOT available at this workflow key. The `hashFiles` function cannot be used here.
```
